content: We can frame curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms from autonomous agents as a self-organizing active memory pruning system for cutting-edge 2024-2025 long-context agent memory architectures, where intrinsic reward signals from ICM/RND modules double as importance scores to prioritize storing high-uncertainty, learnable new experiences instead of redundant, low-information content. This replaces static hand-coded memory pruning rules with an agent's own autonomous curiosity to curate what it retains for future active learning.
